chapter_title.ar: بَابٌ فِي أُصُولِ الْكُفْرِ وَ أَرْكَانِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 115 – The origins of the disbelief and its corner-stones

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (0/82)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (0/82)
text: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) ثَلَاثٌ مَلْعُونَاتٌ مَلْعُونٌ مَنْ فَعَلَهُنَّ الْمُتَغَوِّطُ فِي ظِلِّ النُّزَّالِ وَ الْمَانِعُ الْمَاءَ الْمُنْتَابَ وَ السَّادُّ الطَّرِيقَ الْمُعْرَبَةَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Ibrahim Bin Ziyad Al Karkhy,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘Three (things) are cursed. Accursed is the one who does these – The defecation in the shade of encampments (public places), and the prevention of the allocated water, and the blocking of public roads’.
en.sarwar: 11. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Ibrahim ibn Ziyad al-Karkhi from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “The Messenger of Allah has said, ‘Three things are condemned and condemned are those who perform them: Defecating in recreational places, blocking allocated water and blocking public roads.’”
